text: The robotic third arm is positioned on the left side of the patient and is used to retract the liver upwards during the robotic cholecystectomy procedure.

The surgeon uses a hook diathermy to dissect the anterior and posterior aspects of Calot's triangle. The cystic duct is visibly dilated during dissection. A high-rising cystic artery is clipped and divided. Indocyanine green (ICG) is utilized to confirm the anatomy of the cystic duct, common bile duct, and their junction.

The surgeon achieves the critical view of safety and proceeds to dissect the gallbladder from the liver. The cystic duct is opened, and stones are retrieved using upward stroke pressure on the cystic duct and common bile duct.

The surgeon uses a Fogarty catheter to remove debris from the cystic duct. An additional stone impacted in the cystic duct is extracted, and the duct is shortened to facilitate the removal of deeper stones.
An intraoperative cholangiogram is performed, visually assessing the patency of the common bile duct. The surgeon closes the cystic duct stump using a continuous suture with 4-0 PDS material.
